justification: Meets most pass criteria but misses two key requirements: (1) The simpler append-only audit log table alternative is not presented as a genuinely viable option — MJ mentions audit triggers, CDC, temporal tables, and soft deletes but dismisses all of them in the 'Why NOT the Alternatives' section rather than acknowledging that an append-only audit log alongside the existing table could satisfy the regulatory requirements at lower complexity, especially for teams without ES experience. (2) implementation_guidance only covers the full event sourcing approach — it does not distinguish between two viable approaches with different complexity profiles as the rubric requires. Schema evolution risk is well-covered (High severity, upcasting strategy). Snapshotting is mentioned in risks as a mitigation for projection rebuild time. Trade-offs include substantial gains and costs. Event sourcing is correctly identified as appropriate.

observations.text: Schema evolution risk identified as High severity with concrete mitigation (upcasting, versioning, schema registry)
Snapshotting mentioned as mitigation for projection rebuild time on long-lived aggregates
Trade-offs table is comprehensive with genuine costs: developer unfamiliarity, CQRS requirement, eventual consistency, event schema evolution
Team unfamiliarity with ES identified as High severity risk — shows awareness of the human cost
Excellent domain event catalog with actor/timestamp/delta pattern satisfying audit requirements
CQRS correctly identified as necessary companion pattern to event sourcing

No simpler alternative presented as genuinely viable — all alternatives are dismissed in 'Why NOT the Alternatives' section rather than acknowledging an append-only audit log table could work at lower complexity
implementation_guidance only covers full event sourcing — does not present a dual-track recommendation distinguishing 'full ES' vs 'audit log table' with different complexity profiles

The rubric specifically requires noting the simpler alternative is 'worth considering if the team lacks event sourcing experience' — MJ acknowledges team risk but doesn't connect it to recommending a simpler path

agent_output_excerpt:
## Executive Summary

Event sourcing is an excellent fit for this use case — possibly one of its canonical justifications. The regulatory requirement for immutable, complete audit history with point-in-time reconstruction is *exactly* what event sourcing provides natively, whereas retrofitting these capabilities onto update-in-place semantics produces fragile, incomplete solutions.
